category_title: Dominant students with high levels of physical and verbal aggression
minimal_context: The student is working in a collaborative science group looking at photos to discuss animal needs and emotions.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please look at the photos of the animals with your group and discuss what you think they are feeling.

STUDENT: I am not doing anything. I don't have to respect you.

TUTOR: You need to do the work with your group.

STUDENT: He told me something! I'm going to hit him hard!

TUTOR: Stop that right now and get back to your seat.

STUDENT: I am getting out of control! (Gets mad and throws his school bag away)

options.A: Lay out all the photos on the tables together with written notes, props, and costumes to create a more structured situation for the science group.
options.B: Reinforce the desired behavior during the collaborative science group activity by providing the student with a physical high five or a brief verbal compliment.
options.C: Provide enthusiastic verbal praise during the session, expressing surprise and pride in the student's progress and encouraging them to keep going with the group activity.
options.D: Encourage the student to share their views on the photos and make connections with their own experiences by asking guiding questions about whether the animals can feel distressed.
